classes.name: Algebra 1
classes.subject: math
classes.description: Foundation course covering linear equations, inequalities, functions, and graphing. Essential for all future math courses.

classes.name: Geometry
classes.subject: math
classes.description: Study of shapes, proofs, spatial reasoning, and geometric properties. Includes both theoretical and practical applications.

classes.name: Algebra 2
classes.subject: math
classes.description: Advanced algebraic concepts including polynomials, exponential functions, logarithms, and trigonometry basics.

classes.name: Pre-Calculus
classes.subject: math
classes.description: Preparation for calculus with focus on advanced functions, trigonometry, vectors, and mathematical modeling.

classes.name: AP Calculus AB
classes.subject: math
classes.description: College-level calculus covering limits, derivatives, integrals, and their applications. Prepares for AP exam.

classes.name: AP Statistics
classes.subject: math
classes.description: Statistical reasoning, data analysis, probability, and inference. Highly applicable to real-world scenarios.

classes.name: English 9
classes.subject: english
classes.description: Introduction to literary analysis through short stories, novels, poetry, and drama. Focus on essay writing and grammar.

classes.name: English 10
classes.subject: english
classes.description: World literature with emphasis on cultural perspectives. Develops research and argumentative writing skills.

classes.name: American Literature
classes.subject: english
classes.description: Survey of American authors from colonial period to present. Analysis of historical context and literary movements.

classes.name: AP English Literature
classes.subject: english
classes.description: College-level analysis of poetry, prose, and drama. Heavy reading load with sophisticated analytical essays.

classes.name: AP English Language
classes.subject: english
classes.description: Rhetoric and composition focused on non-fiction analysis. Develops persuasive and analytical writing skills.

classes.name: Creative Writing
classes.subject: english
classes.description: Workshop-style class exploring fiction, poetry, and creative non-fiction. Emphasis on peer feedback and revision.

classes.name: World History
classes.subject: history
classes.description: Global history from ancient civilizations to modern times. Focus on cultural exchange and major historical movements.

classes.name: AP World History
classes.subject: history
classes.description: College-level world history with extensive document analysis and essay writing. Fast-paced content coverage.

classes.name: US History
classes.subject: history
classes.description: American history from colonial era through present day. Required for graduation in most states.

classes.name: AP US History
classes.subject: history
classes.description: Rigorous examination of American history with heavy emphasis on primary source analysis and DBQs.

classes.name: Government
classes.subject: history
classes.description: American government structure, political processes, and civics. Often includes current events discussions.

classes.name: Economics
classes.subject: history
classes.description: Introduction to microeconomics and macroeconomics. Practical applications to personal finance and markets.

classes.name: Biology
classes.subject: science
classes.description: Study of living organisms, cells, genetics, evolution, and ecology. Includes lab work and dissections.

classes.name: AP Biology
classes.subject: science
classes.description: College-level biology with extensive lab work. Heavy content load covering molecular biology to ecosystems.

classes.name: Chemistry
classes.subject: science
classes.description: Study of matter, atomic structure, chemical reactions, and stoichiometry. Math-intensive with regular lab experiments.

classes.name: AP Chemistry
classes.subject: science
classes.description: Advanced chemistry with college-level rigor. Significant mathematical problem solving and complex lab reports.

classes.name: Physics
classes.subject: science
classes.description: Classical mechanics, electricity, magnetism, and waves. Math-heavy with practical lab applications.

classes.name: AP Physics 1
classes.subject: science
classes.description: Algebra-based physics covering mechanics, energy, and waves. Conceptual understanding with mathematical application.

classes.name: Environmental Science
classes.subject: science
classes.description: Study of ecosystems, sustainability, and human environmental impact. Field work and community projects.

classes.name: Spanish 1
classes.subject: language
classes.description: Introduction to Spanish language and culture. Focus on basic conversation, grammar, and vocabulary.

classes.name: Spanish 2
classes.subject: language
classes.description: Continued development of Spanish skills with more complex grammar and expanded vocabulary.

classes.name: Spanish 3
classes.subject: language
classes.description: Advanced Spanish with emphasis on literature, composition, and fluent conversation.

classes.name: AP Spanish Language
classes.subject: language
classes.description: College-level Spanish emphasizing communication, cultural understanding, and authentic materials.

classes.name: French 1
classes.subject: language
classes.description: Beginning French language and Francophone culture. Speaking, listening, reading, and writing skills.

classes.name: French 2
classes.subject: language
classes.description: Continued French study with increased emphasis on conversation and cultural exploration.

classes.name: Studio Art
classes.subject: arts
classes.description: Exploration of various art media including drawing, painting, and sculpture. Build foundational art skills.

classes.name: AP Studio Art
classes.subject: arts
classes.description: Portfolio-based course developing sustained investigation and artistic voice. Requires significant independent work.

classes.name: Music Theory
classes.subject: arts
classes.description: Study of musical structure, harmony, rhythm, and composition. Great for aspiring musicians.

classes.name: Concert Band
classes.subject: arts
classes.description: Performance-based class learning and performing diverse musical repertoire. Regular concerts and events.

classes.name: Introduction to Programming
classes.subject: pathways
classes.description: Learn programming fundamentals using Python. No prior experience required.

classes.name: AP Computer Science A
classes.subject: pathways
classes.description: College-level computer science using Java. Object-oriented programming, data structures, and algorithms.

classes.name: Web Development
classes.subject: pathways
classes.description: Build websites using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. Create portfolio of web projects.

classes.name: Physical Education
classes.subject: pe
classes.description: Lifetime fitness activities, team sports, and health education. Required for graduation.

classes.name: Team Sports
classes.subject: pe
classes.description: Competitive team activities including basketball, soccer, volleyball, and flag football.
